source: Predicting Twitter Engagement With Deep Language Models

concepts.finding: Deep language models for predicting Twitter engagement (likes, retweets, replies). Tweet representation via: (1) BERT for text content, (2) user embeddings (historical engagement patterns), (3) topic embeddings (LDA on tweet corpus). Multi-task learning predicts multiple engagement types simultaneously. Text content is the strongest predictor; user identity adds 15% improvement.
concepts.implication: Keyword engagement prediction: deep language model for keyword text + frequency/reach features + topic distribution predicts keyword engagement with intents. Multi-task across intent dimensions improves per-dimension accuracy.
concepts.open_question: Can deep language models predict keyword intent membership from text alone, without co-occurrence data? How good is zero-shot keyword-to-intent assignment?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: BERT-based zero-shot keyword-to-intent prediction (using only keyword text and intent descriptions) achieves 60%+ accuracy, significantly above random baseline, enabling cold-start keyword handling.
